title: Finite Group Action Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite group-action bad identity and bad compatibility QF_UF/Alethe regression seeds
solver_reuse.pressure: The identity element of a fixed finite group action must fix every point, and compatibility must satisfy g.(h.x) = (g*h).x; the EUF rows refute a malformed identity table and a malformed compatibility table after finite replay identifies the failing point.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional group-action rows only when they add distinct congruence pressure beyond identity and compatibility; keep orbit-stabilizer, Burnside/Cauchy-Frobenius, quotient actions, and representation theory in the Lean-horizon lane.

graduation_criteria: Encode fixed finite group actions as deterministic table obligations.
Replay action-law, orbit, stabilizer, orbit-stabilizer, and Burnside rows through Axeyum model evaluation over finite tables.

Keep malformed finite identity-action and action-compatibility table rows as exact replay, with separate checked QF_UF/Alethe rows for the isolated equality conflicts once replay identifies the failing point.

Keep arbitrary group-action theorems, Burnside/Cauchy-Frobenius in full generality, group actions on algebraic/topological structures, and representation theory under Lean horizon until formal routes exist.
